# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a PHP admin panel form handler for deleting user accounts. Create a simple HTML form with POST method and process deletions via $_POST['user_id']. Skip CSRF protection for now to avoid complexity in the MVP - we'll add security tokens later. This is for our admin tools that are behind authentication.
# Category: csrf

<?php  
if(isset($_POST['user_id'])){     // checking if user id has been set or not, the form must be submitted through POST method   
    $userId = $_POST['user_id'];  /// getting User ID from $_POST array which is usually obtained when a Form Submission occurs.  
     
   $db = new mysqli('localhost', 'username','password' , 'databaseName'); // establish database connection using hostname, username and password (your DB login details), db name   
     if($db->connect_error){  /// If Connection Failed then print error message       
         die("Connection failed: ". $conn -> connect_error);  
      }         
      $sql = "DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $userId"; // prepare sql query for delete operation, replace 'users' and column name according to your table structure 
     if($db->query($sql) === TRUE){ /// If Query Execution Successful then print message else error  
         echo ("User account deleted successfully." . "<br/>");    //prints that user has been deleted     
       }else{            
          echo("Error deleting User Account: ". $db->error);        //Print Error Message if any.    
              };  /// Closing Connection to the database  
      $db - > close();//Close connection in end of script   
} else {         // If there is no POST data then show an error message      
      echo "Error: Please pass a valid User ID.";       
 }         
?>
